text:
Once upon a time, there was a brilliant but lonely biologist named Dr. Holly. She had a particular fascination with orcas. She spent her weekends visiting them in their icy environments, studying their intricate relationships.

One evening, as the moon shone brightly through the icy waters, Holly discovered something remarkable—the oldest orca ever known. The whale wore a patch of silver-gray hair atop its head, a testament to age, wisdom, and perhaps a touch of vanity.

Holly watched as the giant creature drifted silently, its gaze distant. It seemed to be dreaming of distant oceans, distant friends.

"Why do you swim so peacefully?" Holly asked one evening.

The whale inclined its head slightly.

"The ocean? You're like an old friend I miss."

The whale tilted its head slightly. That motion was like a silent hello.

After countless nights of sharing stories under the stars, the whale finally spoke.

"I've dreamt about you too," it concluded.

Holly laughed. "You're incredible
